questions.question: Explain the term biodiversity.
questions.answer: Biodiversity refers to the variety of plant and animal species present in a particular region.

questions.question: Why is India considered a mega biodiversity country?
questions.answer: India is considered a mega biodiversity country because it has a wide variety of plant and animal species.

questions.question: Explain two characteristics of tropical evergreen forests.
questions.answer: They receive heavy rainfall and have dense vegetation that remains green throughout the year.

questions.question: Explain two features of tropical deciduous forests.
questions.answer: Trees shed leaves in the dry season and the forests depend mainly on monsoon rainfall.

questions.question: Explain two adaptations of plants in thorn forests.
questions.answer: Plants have long roots to reach water and small leaves to reduce evaporation.

questions.question: Why does vegetation change with altitude in mountains?
questions.answer: Vegetation changes with altitude due to variations in temperature and rainfall.

questions.question: Explain the importance of mangrove forests.
questions.answer: Mangrove forests protect coastal areas from erosion and support biodiversity.

questions.question: Name two animals found in Himalayan regions.
questions.answer: Yak and snow leopard.

questions.question: Why are forests important for wildlife?
questions.answer: Forests provide food, shelter and breeding places for animals.

questions.question: Explain the role of forests in maintaining ecological balance.
questions.answer: Forests regulate climate, conserve soil and support biodiversity.

questions.question: What is the habitat of the Asiatic lion?
questions.answer: Gir Forest in Gujarat.

questions.question: Why are medicinal plants important?
questions.answer: They are used to prepare medicines to treat various diseases.

questions.question: Give two causes of biodiversity loss.
questions.answer: Deforestation and hunting.

questions.question: Why are biosphere reserves established?
questions.answer: They are established to protect biodiversity and natural ecosystems.

questions.question: Name two biosphere reserves in India.
questions.answer: Nilgiri and Sundarbans.

questions.question: Explain the role of Project Tiger.
questions.answer: Project Tiger was launched to protect the tiger population and conserve its habitat.

questions.question: Why are national parks important?
questions.answer: National parks protect wildlife and preserve natural ecosystems.

questions.question: Why are wildlife sanctuaries created?
questions.answer: Wildlife sanctuaries are created to conserve animals in their natural habitat.

questions.question: Name two migratory birds that visit India.
questions.answer: Siberian crane and flamingo.

questions.question: Explain the importance of conservation of forests.
questions.answer: Conservation of forests protects biodiversity and maintains ecological balance.

questions.question: Why is hunting harmful to wildlife?
questions.answer: Hunting reduces animal populations and may lead to extinction.

questions.question: Why should forests not be cut indiscriminately?
questions.answer: Because it destroys habitats and disturbs ecological balance.

questions.question: Explain one role of insects in ecosystems.
questions.answer: Insects help in pollination of plants.

questions.question: Why are wetlands important for migratory birds?
questions.answer: Wetlands provide food and shelter for migratory birds.

questions.question: Explain the importance of biodiversity conservation.
questions.answer: Biodiversity conservation ensures survival of species and maintains ecological balance.
